feedback_addressed: Added comprehensive interest rate sensitivity table: per-REIT price impact at +50bps, +100bps, and -50bps with AFFO impact
Added P/NAV analysis for every BUY-rated REIT: EQIX 86.2%, AMT 80.8%, DLR 92.5%, VTR 93%, AVB 83.8%, EQR 89.2%, VICI 84.1%
Added data center supply-demand analysis: 100 GW planned additions 2026-2030 against 100 GW current capacity, 97% occupancy
Added debt maturity schedules for leveraged picks: VTR $2.06B maturing 2026, EQIX $1.3B in 2027
Added construction cost inflation impact: +43% since 2020 constraining new supply and supporting existing portfolio values
Reduced data center concentration from 30% to 20%
Widened scoring spread from 5.8-8.1 to 3.8-8.5 improving discrimination
Removed HOLD-rated names -- every pick is now BUY or SELL with clear action
Applied EQIX overvaluation flag consistently alongside WELL (same standard)
Added AMT CoreSite revenue contribution analysis
Added rent regulation risk for apartment REITs by market
Added position sizing with entry/exit levels
feedback_ignored: VICI mark-to-market opportunity analysis on 40-year leases still lacks specific quantification of how much below market the locked-in rents are
remaining_flaws: Data center overbuild risk is acknowledged (doubling capacity) but the base case still assumes strong demand -- the report could more explicitly model a scenario where 20-30% of planned capacity is cancelled or delayed
SBAC and CCI are mentioned in the rate sensitivity analysis as SELL candidates but their full SELL thesis is not as developed as NEE in utilities or MP in materials

The 97% data center occupancy rate may not persist through a capacity doubling -- historical occupancy during rapid buildout periods for other REIT subsectors (industrial 2021-2023) would provide useful analog

The v2 report started from a higher base (73) and shows the smallest improvement delta (+7), suggesting this sector needed less revision but also that the analyst may have been less aggressive in self-correction
what_impressed: P/NAV analysis for every BUY-rated REIT using cap rate methodology provides the fundamental valuation metric that was almost entirely missing in v1
AMT at 19% discount to NAV identified as the deepest value among picks, with CoreSite worth $30/share (14% of NAV) -- genuinely actionable finding
Interest rate sensitivity differentiating between price impact and AFFO impact shows understanding that sentiment and fundamentals respond differently to rates
Construction cost inflation (+43% since 2020) as a structural barrier to new supply supporting existing REIT portfolios is a key insight for the investment thesis
Consistent overvaluation standard applied to EQIX (not just WELL) addresses the v1 criticism about inconsistent standards
Debt maturity schedules for VTR ($2.06B in 2026) and refinancing risk analysis provide the leverage data that was missing
